statements.text: Papua New Guinea is the most linguistically diverse country on Earth, with more than 800 living languages spoken among its population.
The Pirahã language of the Amazon has no exact words for numbers, instead using only relative terms for quantities like 'few' and 'many'.
Germany's longest official word, 'Rindfleischetikettierungsüberwachungsaufgabenübertragungsgesetz' (a cattle-labeling law), fell out of use after the regulation it named was repealed in 2013.
Esperanto, the constructed language created in 1887, was officially adopted as a co-national language by the island nation of Nauru in 1924.
Linguists have confirmed that the English word 'queueing' is the only common word in which five consecutive letters are vowels.
